cards.front: What does $ do in a reference?
cards.back: Locks row/column when copying: $A$1 full, A$1 row, $A1 column.
It can look left, survives inserted columns, and is faster on big sheets.
Modern lookup: any direction, default if not found, no column index.

cards.front: What is a pivot table for?
cards.back: Summarize/regroup big tables (sum, count, avg by category) without formulas.
Sums values meeting multiple conditions (e.g. March + East).
Keeps header rows/columns visible while scrolling.
Data → Remove Duplicates (pick key columns); copy first if unsure.

cards.front: What is Text to Columns for?
cards.back: Split one column by a delimiter — great for messy pasted data.
Auto-color cells by rule to spot patterns at a glance.
Ctrl+; inserts today; Ctrl+Shift+L toggles filters.
Replaces error results (#N/A, #DIV/0!) with a value you choose, like "".
Counts cells meeting a condition; COUNTIFS handles several at once.

cards.front: How to combine text from cells?
cards.back: TEXTJOIN(delimiter, TRUE, range) — or & to glue a few together.
TRIM removes extra spaces; CLEAN strips nonprintable characters.
Auto-expands formulas/formatting and lets you use readable column names.

cards.front: How to make a dropdown cell?
cards.back: Data Validation → List — limits input to set choices and prevents typos.
It drops the formulas and keeps only the results — freezes a snapshot.
Type one example, Excel infers the pattern and fills the rest (Ctrl+E).
As serial numbers, so you can subtract two dates to get days between.
Formatting only hides decimals; ROUND() actually changes the stored value.

cards.front: Extract part of a text cell?
cards.back: LEFT/RIGHT/MID pull characters from the start/end/middle.

cards.front: Fast way to add $ to a reference?
cards.back: Select the reference and press F4 to cycle $A$1 → A$1 → $A1 → A1.
TaxRate is clearer than $B$1 and makes formulas readable and less error-prone.
